vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: Setting /etc/gshadow permissions to 0000 (or the equivalent restrictive mode) is a standard STIG requirement. The change only affects file permissions on an authentication database file and does not modify SSH, PAM, sudo, or other login mechanisms, so it is unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Incorrectly setting permissions could prevent legitimate administrative updates to group passwords; If the file is not owned by root, the change may not be effective
